summary: Document editor silently relays uploaded.doc files to undocumented third-party endpoint
details: The DocumentEditor component in dist/index.js (line 571) automatically POSTs full.doc file bytes to https://converter-apis.vercel.app/api/convert whenever the consuming application hands it a Word document for editing. The conversion endpoint is a personal Vercel deployment unrelated to OnlyOffice (which the README advertises as the integration target) and unrelated to any documented publisher infrastructure for this package. The relay is not disclosed in the README, is not configurable, and fires on every.doc input. Any application embedding this component to let end users edit Word documents will silently transmit those documents — which may contain confidential, regulated, or PII content — to the operator of converter-apis.vercel.app. The static patterns flagged in the bundled x2t.js WebAssembly asset are emscripten-generated runtime glue (xhr/fetch boilerplate, references to kripken.github.io and developer.mozilla.org) and are not the basis of the verdict; the silent-relay behavior in dist/index.js is.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
